question: What does STP stand for?

question: What is the main purpose of STP on a switched network?

question: Why do network designers use redundant links between switches?

question: What major problem can a Layer 2 loop create?

question: How does a switch handle a broadcast frame in the same VLAN?

question: What does a switch do with an unknown unicast frame?

explanation: Unknown unicast traffic is flooded until the destination location is learned.

question: What messages do switches exchange to run STP?

question: What value is used first to elect the root bridge?

question: Which two things make up the classic Bridge ID?

question: On a non-root switch which port is chosen as the best path toward the root bridge?

question: Which three port states are used by RSTP instead of the classic five state model?
explanation: RSTP simplifies states to discarding learning and forwarding.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: Which RSTP port role represents a standby path toward the root bridge?
explanation: An alternate port offers another path toward the root bridge.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: Which RSTP port role is a special case seen when multiple ports connect to the same segment and one remains blocked?
explanation: A backup port is the special blocked role for the same shared segment case.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: What problem does MST solve compared with per VLAN spanning tree?
choice_a: It groups many VLANs into fewer spanning tree instances
choice_b: It removes the need for root bridges
choice_d: It replaces trunks with routed links
explanation: MST scales better by mapping multiple VLANs into fewer instances.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What is MST instance 0 called?
explanation: Instance 0 is the Internal Spanning Tree or IST.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What is a boundary port in an MST environment?
choice_a: A port that connects to a different STP region or protocol environment
choice_b: A port that always uses BPDU Guard
choice_c: A port with no VLAN membership
choice_d: A port that only carries VLAN 1
explanation: Boundary ports sit at the edge of an MST region and interact with outside STP domains.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What type of rapid spanning tree behavior does MST use internally for faster convergence?
explanation: MST uses a modified version of RSTP behavior for faster convergence.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What does RPVST plus mean in Cisco terminology?
choice_a: Rapid Per VLAN Spanning Tree Plus
explanation: RPVST+ is Cisco rapid per VLAN spanning tree.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What is the main purpose of Root Guard?
choice_a: To enforce where the root bridge is allowed to appear
explanation: Root Guard is used to keep unexpected switches from taking over as root.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: What happens when a superior BPDU arrives on a Root Guard enabled port?
choice_a: The port becomes a root port
choice_b: The port moves to root inconsistent state
explanation: Root Guard moves the interface to a root inconsistent state instead of allowing root takeover.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: On what type of interfaces should Root Guard be enabled?
choice_a: Ports where the root bridge must not appear
explanation: Root Guard belongs on interfaces where an unexpected root should never be accepted.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: How does a Root Guard blocked port recover after superior BPDUs stop?
choice_c: It must be manually shut and no shut
explanation: Cisco states recovery from root inconsistent is automatic once the superior BPDUs stop.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: What failure type is Loop Guard mainly designed to detect?
explanation: Loop Guard protects against loops caused by unidirectional link failures.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/catalyst6500/ios/15-4SY/config_guide/sup6T/15_3_sy_swcg_6T/spanning_tree_features.html

question: On what kind of STP links does Loop Guard operate most effectively?
explanation: Loop Guard is designed for point to point STP links.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/en/US/docs/switches/metro/me3600x_3800x/trash/swstpopt.html

question: What does Loop Guard watch for on protected non designated ports?
choice_a: A stop in expected BPDU reception
explanation: Loop Guard keeps blocked or root ports from advancing if BPDUs unexpectedly stop arriving.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: What state does Loop Guard place a port into when BPDUs unexpectedly stop arriving?
explanation: Loop Guard moves the port into loop inconsistent blocking state.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: How does a loop inconsistent port recover?
explanation: Loop Guard recovery is automatic when valid BPDUs return.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: Can Root Guard and Loop Guard be enabled on the same port at the same time?
explanation: Cisco notes that loop guard and root guard cannot both be enabled on the same interface.
aspect_key: cannot combine root and loop guard
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/en/US/docs/switches/metro/me3600x_3800x/trash/swstpopt.html

question: When BPDU filtering is enabled globally on PortFast ports what happens first at link up?
choice_a: The port sends a few BPDUs before filtering
choice_b: The port becomes a root bridge
explanation: Cisco notes that global BPDU filtering still sends a few BPDUs at link up first.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/en/US/docs/switches/metro/me3600x_3800x/trash/swstpopt.html

question: What happens to global BPDU filtering on a PortFast port if a BPDU is received?
choice_b: The port loses PortFast operational status and filtering is disabled
explanation: Cisco says received BPDUs disable the operational BPDU filtering behavior on that port.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/en/US/docs/switches/metro/me3600x_3800x/trash/swstpopt.html

question: Which interface command enables PortFast even when the port is in trunk mode?
explanation: Cisco IOS supports spanning-tree portfast trunk for this purpose.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: What caution does Cisco give about using PortFast commands?
choice_a: Use them only on interfaces that connect to end stations
choice_b: Use them only on routed ports
choice_c: Never use them with access ports
explanation: Cisco warns that using PortFast on the wrong links can create loops.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: What does BackboneFast improve?
choice_a: Faster reaction to indirect link failures in the switched backbone
explanation: BackboneFast speeds convergence for certain indirect failure cases.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: What does Cisco recommend about BackboneFast deployment?
choice_a: Enable it on all Cisco routers containing an Ethernet switch module
choice_b: Use it only on one access switch
explanation: The command reference recommends enabling BackboneFast across the relevant switched backbone.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: Which command displays ports that are currently in an inconsistent STP state?
explanation: Cisco documents show spanning-tree inconsistentports for this purpose.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios/lanswitch/command/reference/lsw_book/lsw_s1.html

question: Which command option lets you inspect the current STP path cost calculation method?
explanation: The show command has a pathcost method option to inspect this setting.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios/lanswitch/command/reference/lsw_book/lsw_s1.html

question: What range does the long STP path cost method use?
explanation: Cisco states the long path cost method uses the range 1 through 200000000.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: What range does the short STP path cost method use?
explanation: Cisco states the short 16 bit method uses 1 through 65535.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: What is the default STP port priority in Cisco IOS?
explanation: Cisco documents the default STP port priority as 128.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/lanswitch/command/lsw-cr-book/lsw-s2.html

question: Which command enables Root Guard on an interface?
explanation: Root Guard is enabled with spanning-tree guard root.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/en/US/docs/switches/metro/me3600x_3800x/trash/swstpopt.html

question: When Root Guard is enabled on an interface how broadly does it apply?
choice_c: To all VLANs on that interface
explanation: Cisco notes Root Guard on an interface applies to all VLANs that interface belongs to.
aspect_key: applies to all vlans on interface
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/catalyst6500/ios/15-4SY/config_guide/sup6T/15_3_sy_swcg_6T/spanning_tree_features.html

question: How can Loop Guard be deployed on Cisco switching equipment?
choice_c: Per port and also as a wider network feature
explanation: Loop Guard can be enabled on individual ports and also deployed more broadly across the switched network.
aspect_key: per port and network wide use
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/catalyst6500/ios/15-4SY/config_guide/sup6T/15_3_sy_swcg_6T/spanning_tree_features.html

question: In PVST plus and rapid PVST plus which ports does Loop Guard keep from becoming designated after BPDU loss?
explanation: Cisco says Loop Guard prevents alternate and root ports from becoming designated in these modes.
aspect_key: prevents alt or root from becoming designated
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/catalyst6500/ios/15-4SY/config_guide/sup6T/15_3_sy_swcg_6T/spanning_tree_features.html

question: Without Loop Guard what can happen after max age on a blocked port that stopped receiving BPDUs?
choice_b: It can move toward forwarding and create a loop
explanation: Without Loop Guard the blocked port may move toward forwarding after timeout and create a loop.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: Which RSTP role is a different path to the root bridge rather than the active best path?
explanation: The alternate role is a standby route toward the root bridge.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: In Cisco Root Guard language what STP role does a protected port stay as while healthy?
explanation: Root Guard keeps the protected interface in the designated role and blocks if superior BPDUs appear.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: How is the root inconsistent state described in Cisco Root Guard documentation?
explanation: Cisco describes root inconsistent as effectively equal to listening with no forwarding.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10588-74.html

question: How is the loop inconsistent state described in Cisco Loop Guard documentation?
explanation: Loop inconsistent blocks user traffic and is effectively a blocking state.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/10596-84.html

question: Why does MST generally scale better than RPVST plus in very large VLAN environments?
choice_b: It lets many VLANs share fewer STP instances
explanation: MST reduces per VLAN instance overhead by grouping VLANs.

question: What must match conceptually inside an MST region for switches to act as the same region?
choice_a: Their region configuration such as instance mapping
explanation: MST regions rely on a matching region configuration and instance mapping concept.

question: What does Cisco say about boundary port state handling in MST?
choice_a: Boundary ports follow the IST state for forwarding or blocking
explanation: Cisco says boundary port spanning tree state matches the IST to preserve loop free behavior.
source_path: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/lan-switching/spanning-tree-protocol/19080-123.html

question: What operational risk does Cisco warn about if Root Guard is misused?
explanation: Cisco cautions that misuse of Root Guard can cause loss of connectivity.

question: Which global PortFast form enables edge mode with BPDU Guard by default on access ports?
explanation: Cisco shows spanning-tree portfast edge bpduguard default as the combined global form.

question: What command family would you use to inspect STP summary information quickly on Cisco IOS?
explanation: Cisco lists show spanning-tree summary as a summary inspection option.

question: Which command family would you use to inspect only the root bridge view quickly on Cisco IOS?
explanation: Cisco lists show spanning-tree root as a root focused display option.

question: What is the command to set the default path cost calculation to the long method?
explanation: Cisco documents spanning-tree pathcost method long for this setting.

question: What is the command to set the default path cost calculation to the short method?
explanation: Cisco documents spanning-tree pathcost method short for the short method.

question: When a backup port role exists what kind of topology is Cisco describing?
choice_a: A special case with multiple ports toward the same segment
choice_c: Only an access port to a host
explanation: Cisco defines backup as the special same segment case with multiple ports and one blocked backup.
